lines.english: Lesson 30
lines.chinese: 第30课
lines.raw: Lesson 30 | 第30课

lines.english: Exploring the sea-floor
lines.chinese: 探索海底
lines.raw: Exploring the sea-floor | 探索海底

lines.english: How did people probably imagine the sea-floor before it was investigated?
lines.chinese: 在海底被勘测之前，人们可能如何想象它？
lines.raw: How did people probably imagine the sea-floor before it was investigated? | 在海底被勘测之前，人们可能如何想象它？

lines.english: Our knowledge of the oceans a hundred years ago was confined to the two-dimensional shape of the sea surface
lines.chinese: 一百年前，我们对海洋的了解仅限于海面的二维形状
lines.raw: Our knowledge of the oceans a hundred years ago was confined to the two-dimensional shape of the sea surface | 一百年前，我们对海洋的了解仅限于海面的二维形状

lines.english: and the hazards of navigation presented by the irregularities in depth of the shallow water close to the land.
lines.chinese: 以及陆地附近浅水区深度不规则所带来的航行危险。
lines.raw: and the hazards of navigation presented by the irregularities in depth of the shallow water close to the land. | 以及陆地附近浅水区深度不规则所带来的航行危险。

lines.english: The open sea was deep and mysterious,
lines.chinese: 开阔的海洋深邃而神秘，
lines.raw: The open sea was deep and mysterious, | 开阔的海洋深邃而神秘，

lines.english: and anyone who gave more than a passing thought to the bottom confines of the oceans probably assumed that the sea-bed was flat.
lines.chinese: 任何对海洋底部边界稍加思考的人可能都认为海床是平坦的。
lines.raw: and anyone who gave more than a passing thought to the bottom confines of the oceans probably assumed that the sea-bed was flat. | 任何对海洋底部边界稍加思考的人可能都认为海床是平坦的。

lines.english: Sir James Clark Ross had obtained a sounding of over 2, 400 fathoms in 1839,
lines.chinese: 詹姆斯·克拉克·罗斯爵士于1839年测得超过2400噚的深度，
lines.raw: Sir James Clark Ross had obtained a sounding of over 2, 400 fathoms in 1839, | 詹姆斯·克拉克·罗斯爵士于1839年测得超过2400噚的深度，

lines.english: but it was not until 1869, when H.M.S. Porcupine was put at the disposal of the Royal Society for several cruises
lines.chinese: 但直到1869年，当“豪猪号”军舰被皇家学会征用进行数次航行时，
lines.raw: but it was not until 1869, when H.M.S. Porcupine was put at the disposal of the Royal Society for several cruises | 但直到1869年，当“豪猪号”军舰被皇家学会征用进行数次航行时，

lines.english: that a series of deep soundings was obtained in the Atlantic and the first samples were collected by dredging the bottom.
lines.chinese: 才在大西洋获得了一系列深海探测数据，并通过海底拖网收集了第一批样本。
lines.raw: that a series of deep soundings was obtained in the Atlantic and the first samples were collected by dredging the bottom. | 才在大西洋获得了一系列深海探测数据，并通过海底拖网收集了第一批样本。

lines.english: Shortly after this the famous H.M.S. Challenger expedition established the study of the sea-floor
lines.chinese: 此后不久，著名的“挑战者号”军舰远征队确立了海底研究
lines.raw: Shortly after this the famous H.M.S. Challenger expedition established the study of the sea-floor | 此后不久，著名的“挑战者号”军舰远征队确立了海底研究

lines.english: as a subject worthy of the most qualified physicists and geologists.
lines.chinese: 成为值得最合格的物理学家和地质学家研究的课题。
lines.raw: as a subject worthy of the most qualified physicists and geologists. | 成为值得最合格的物理学家和地质学家研究的课题。

lines.english: A burst of activity associated with the laying of submarine cables
lines.chinese: 随之而来的海底电缆铺设热潮
lines.raw: A burst of activity associated with the laying of submarine cables | 随之而来的海底电缆铺设热潮

lines.english: soon confirmed the Challenger's observation that many parts of the ocean were two to three miles deep,
lines.chinese: 很快证实了“挑战者号”的观测结果：海洋许多地方深达两到三英里，
lines.raw: soon confirmed the Challenger's observation that many parts of the ocean were two to three miles deep, | 很快证实了“挑战者号”的观测结果：海洋许多地方深达两到三英里，

lines.english: and the existence of underwater features of considerable magnitude.
lines.chinese: 并存在着规模巨大的水下特征。
lines.raw: and the existence of underwater features of considerable magnitude. | 并存在着规模巨大的水下特征。

lines.english: Today, enough soundings are available to enable a relief map of the Atlantic to be drawn
lines.chinese: 如今，已有的测深数据足以绘制出大西洋的浮雕地图，
lines.raw: Today, enough soundings are available to enable a relief map of the Atlantic to be drawn | 如今，已有的测深数据足以绘制出大西洋的浮雕地图，

lines.english: and we know something of the great variety of the sea bed's topography.
lines.chinese: 我们对海底地形的巨大多样性有了一定的了解。
lines.raw: and we know something of the great variety of the sea bed's topography. | 我们对海底地形的巨大多样性有了一定的了解。

lines.english: Since the sea covers the greater part of the earth's surface,
lines.chinese: 由于海洋覆盖了地球表面的大部分，
lines.raw: Since the sea covers the greater part of the earth's surface, | 由于海洋覆盖了地球表面的大部分，

lines.english: it is quite reasonable to regard the sea floor as the basic form of the crust of the earth,
lines.chinese: 将海底视为地壳的基本形态是相当合理的，
lines.raw: it is quite reasonable to regard the sea floor as the basic form of the crust of the earth, | 将海底视为地壳的基本形态是相当合理的，

lines.english: with superimposed upon it the continents,
lines.chinese: 大陆、
lines.raw: with superimposed upon it the continents, | 大陆、

lines.english: together with the islands and other features of the oceans.
lines.chinese: 以及岛屿和海洋的其他特征都叠加其上。
lines.raw: together with the islands and other features of the oceans. | 以及岛屿和海洋的其他特征都叠加其上。

lines.english: The continents form rugged tablelands which stand nearly three miles above the floor of the open ocean.
lines.chinese: 大陆形成了崎岖不平的高原，它们比开阔海洋的海底高出近三英里。
lines.raw: The continents form rugged tablelands which stand nearly three miles above the floor of the open ocean. | 大陆形成了崎岖不平的高原，它们比开阔海洋的海底高出近三英里。

lines.english: From the shore line, out to a distance which may be anywhere from a few miles to a few hundred miles,
lines.chinese: 从海岸线向外延伸，距离可能从几英里到几百英里不等，
lines.raw: From the shore line, out to a distance which may be anywhere from a few miles to a few hundred miles, | 从海岸线向外延伸，距离可能从几英里到几百英里不等，

lines.english: runs the gentle slope of the continental shelf, geologically part of the continents.
lines.chinese: 是大陆架的平缓斜坡，它在地质学上属于大陆的一部分。
lines.raw: runs the gentle slope of the continental shelf, geologically part of the continents. | 是大陆架的平缓斜坡，它在地质学上属于大陆的一部分。

lines.english: The real dividing line between continents and oceans occurs at the foot of a steeper slope.
lines.chinese: 大陆和海洋的真正分界线出现在一个更陡峭的斜坡底部。
lines.raw: The real dividing line between continents and oceans occurs at the foot of a steeper slope. | 大陆和海洋的真正分界线出现在一个更陡峭的斜坡底部。

lines.english: This continental slope usually starts at a place somewhere near the 100-fathom mark
lines.chinese: 这个大陆坡通常始于100噚标记附近的某个地方，
lines.raw: This continental slope usually starts at a place somewhere near the 100-fathom mark | 这个大陆坡通常始于100噚标记附近的某个地方，

lines.english: and in the course of a few hundred miles reaches the true ocean floor at 2, 500-3, 500 fathoms.
lines.chinese: 并在几百英里的延伸中到达2500-3500噚的真正海底。
lines.raw: and in the course of a few hundred miles reaches the true ocean floor at 2, 500-3, 500 fathoms. | 并在几百英里的延伸中到达2500-3500噚的真正海底。

lines.english: The slope averages about 1 in 30, but contains steep,
lines.chinese: 该斜坡平均坡度约为1:30，但包含陡峭的、
lines.raw: The slope averages about 1 in 30, but contains steep, | 该斜坡平均坡度约为1:30，但包含陡峭的、

lines.english: probably vertical, cliffs, and gentle sediment-covered terraces,
lines.chinese: 可能是垂直的悬崖，以及平缓的被沉积物覆盖的阶地，
lines.raw: probably vertical, cliffs, and gentle sediment-covered terraces, | 可能是垂直的悬崖，以及平缓的被沉积物覆盖的阶地，

lines.english: and near its lower reaches there is a long tailing-off which is almost certainly the result of
lines.chinese: 在其下部区域，有一个长长的逐渐减缓的坡段，这几乎肯定是由于
lines.raw: and near its lower reaches there is a long tailing-off which is almost certainly the result of | 在其下部区域，有一个长长的逐渐减缓的坡段，这几乎肯定是由于

lines.english: material transported out to deep water after being eroded from the continental masses.
lines.chinese: 物质从大陆块体被侵蚀后输送到深水区的结果。
lines.raw: material transported out to deep water after being eroded from the continental masses. | 物质从大陆块体被侵蚀后输送到深水区的结果。
